name: debug-agent-tools
description: Exposes debugging capabilities as Language Model Tools, enabling AI agents to start debug sessions, set breakpoints, step through code, inspect variables, and evaluate expressions

languageModelTools.name: debug_startSession
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Launch a debug session for a program file. Supports any language with a debug adapter installed in VS Code (Stash, Python, Node.js, etc.). Returns a debug snapshot if stopOnEntry is true, or a running status confirmation. Use this before any other debug tools. Only one agent debug session can be active at a time — starting a new one terminates the previous.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Start a debug session for a program

program.description: Absolute path to the file to debug

debugType.description: DAP debug type (e.g. 'python', 'node', 'stash'). If omitted, automatically detected from the file's language by scanning installed debug adapters.

args.description: Command-line arguments to pass to the program

cwd.description: Working directory. Defaults to workspace root.

stopOnEntry.description: Pause on the first line of the program. Default: false.

noDebug.description: Run without debugging (no breakpoints, no stepping). Default: false.

configuration.description: Name of a launch.json configuration to use instead of building an ad-hoc config.

languageModelTools.name: debug_setBreakpoints
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Set breakpoints in a source file. Uses replace semantics — all existing agent-managed breakpoints in the specified file are replaced with the new set. Supports conditional breakpoints, hit count conditions, and logpoints. Works both before and during a debug session. An empty breakpoints array clears all agent breakpoints in the file. Never affects user-set breakpoints.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Set breakpoints in a source file

file.description: Absolute path to the source file

logMessage.description: Log message template (logpoint). Use {expr} for interpolation.

breakpoints.description: Breakpoints to set. Empty array clears all breakpoints in the file.

languageModelTools.name: debug_removeBreakpoints
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Remove agent-managed breakpoints. If a file path is provided, removes only breakpoints in that file. If no file is specified, removes ALL agent-managed breakpoints across all files. Never removes user-set breakpoints.

file.description: Absolute path to the source file. If omitted, removes ALL agent-managed breakpoints.

languageModelTools.name: debug_continue
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Resume program execution. Blocks until the program pauses again (breakpoint hit, exception, or program exits), or until timeout. Returns a debug snapshot with current file, line, call stack, local variables, and captured output. If the program terminates, returns exit status and output. If timeout is reached, returns a running status.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Continue program execution until next pause

threadId.description: Thread to continue. If omitted, continues the currently stopped thread.

timeout.description: Maximum milliseconds to wait for the program to pause. Default: 10000 (10s).

languageModelTools.name: debug_step
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Execute a step operation (over, in, or out) and return the resulting debug snapshot. Step over advances to the next line in the current function. Step in enters a function call. Step out returns to the caller. Can perform multiple steps (up to 20) in one call. Returns a debug snapshot with current file, line, call stack, local variables, and captured output.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Step through code (over, in, or out)

threadId.description: Thread to step. If omitted, steps the currently stopped thread.

count.description: Number of steps to perform. Default: 1. Max: 20.

languageModelTools.name: debug_getSnapshot
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read the current debug state without modifying execution. Returns the current file, line, source context (2 lines before/after), call stack, local variables, closure variables, and captured output. Use variableDepth to expand nested structures (0-3 levels), stackDepth to control stack frame count (1-20), and includeGlobals to see global scope variables.

variableDepth.description: How many levels deep to expand nested variables. Default: 1.

stackDepth.description: Maximum stack frames to include. Default: 5.

includeGlobals.description: Include global scope variables. Default: false.

languageModelTools.name: debug_evaluate
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Evaluate an expression in the context of the current stack frame. Returns the result value and type. Use frameIndex to evaluate in a different stack frame (0 = top/current). The default context is 'watch' (side-effect-free); use 'repl' for expressions that may have side effects (requires user confirmation). If the result is a structured value, it is expanded up to 1 level deep.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Evaluate an expression in the debugger

expression.description: Expression to evaluate in the current frame context

context.description: Evaluation context. 'watch' for side-effect-free, 'repl' for interactive. Default: 'watch'.

languageModelTools.name: debug_stopSession
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Terminate the active agent-managed debug session. Returns a session summary including duration, captured output, and breakpoints hit count. Cleans up all agent-managed breakpoints and event listeners. If no session is active, returns a no_session status.

captureOutput.description: Return stdout/stderr output captured during the session. Default: true.
